title: crepis willdenowii subsp. alikeri
intro: Crepis willdenowii subsp. alikeri is a lesser-known species of the crepis plant, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and native to the Western Ghats in India. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Crepis willdenowii subsp. alikeri include its potential to manage conditions such as diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and certain types of cancer, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote overall well-being and provide relief from various health issues.
